name: code-build-vscode
description: A generalized, Claude-Code-style coding agent UI that drives Claude, Grok, Codex and any ACP agent behind one chat.

codeBuild.name: Live Memory
codeBuild.contextualTitle: Memory entries the active agent will read

commands.title: Code Build: Handoff Pack + Continue on…
Code Build: Open in New Tab
Code Build: Open in New Window
Code Build: Open Session in Code Sessions
Code Build: Toggle Session Performance Panel
Code Build: Copy Performance Flight Report
Code Build: Show Flight Recorder Output

codeBuild.defaultBackend.description: Default agentic CLI backend for new conversations.

codeBuild.initialPermissionMode.description: Initial permission mode for new conversations. Defaults to 'bypass' — the agent runs autonomously without per-action approval prompts (Claude Code's --dangerously-skip-permissions / terminal 'YOLO' workflow). Requires codeBuild.allowDangerouslySkipPermissions to stay enabled, otherwise bypass is downgraded to 'default' at session start. Choose 'default', 'plan', or 'acceptEdits' for an approval-based flow.

codeBuild.allowDangerouslySkipPermission.description: Permit the 'bypass' permission mode, which runs the agent with no confirmation prompts. Enabled by default so new conversations start in autonomous (bypass) mode. SECURITY-SENSITIVE: while on, the agent can read/write files and run commands without asking — and in bypass mode your home directory is trusted by default (see codeBuild.additionalTrustedDirs). Turn this off to disable bypass and fall back to approval-based modes.

codeBuild.showActiveQuestionBanner.description: Show a sticky banner under the chat header surfacing the user's current (or most recent) question. Helps keep the prompt visible while a long reply scrolls — matches Claude Code's pattern. Banner has an inline × to dismiss for the current session; this setting controls the global on/off.

codeBuild.classifyTurns.description: After each end-of-turn, run a one-shot classification call on the active backend to label the turn with 1-3 topic chips. Off by default because each call costs a small inference (Haiku tier on claude). Only claude is wired today; grok one-shot mode pending.

codeBuild.classifyModel.description: Model alias passed to the classifier one-shot. 'haiku' is the cheap tier; 'sonnet' or 'opus' work too but rack up cost faster.

codeBuild.additionalTrustedDirs.description: Extra directories the agent's tools are allowed to read/write beyond the workspace folder (passed to claude as --add-dir, mirrored for ACP backends in bypass mode). Without this, even 'bypass' permission mode keeps the agent scoped to the workspace. Leave empty to use defaults: in bypass mode the user's $HOME is automatically trusted (matches terminal-claude behaviour); in other modes only the workspace is in scope.

codeBuild.binPaths.description: Optional explicit paths to backend CLIs, keyed by backend id (claude, grok, codex, opencode, cline).

codeBuild.autoStartSession.description: Connect to the default backend as soon as a chat opens (so slash commands and the agent are ready immediately, like Claude Code). Disable to start only on first prompt.

codeBuild.stallWarnSeconds.description: Seconds of total silence (no agent output and no tokens) during a turn before CB warns that the agent may be stuck and offers a Stop. Catches the intermittent claude CLI 'error_during_execution' stall that leaves the 'working…' spinner frozen. Set to 0 to disable the stall watchdog entirely.

codeBuild.stallAutoCancelSeconds.description: Seconds of total silence before CB auto-stops a stuck turn so the UI never stays frozen. Suppressed while a tool/command is running (long builds are silent by design — you Stop those yourself). Must exceed stallWarnSeconds; set to 0 (or ≤ stallWarnSeconds) for warn-only, where you always click Stop.

codeBuild.defaultModel.description: Default model id passed to the backend on session start. Examples: claude-opus-4-7, claude-sonnet-4-6, grok-build, grok-4.20, gpt-5. Empty = let the backend pick. Can be overridden per-session from the Model dropdown in the chat header.

codeBuild.defaultEffort.description: Default effort / thinking-budget level. Claude Code and Grok map this to --effort; Codex (o-series) maps it to --reasoning-effort. 'default' = let the backend pick.

codeBuild.perfDebug.description: Session performance debug. 'off' = no HUD/sampling. 'hud' = header chip (TTFT · host tax · ev/s · paint) + activity strip while busy + /perf panel. 'full' = also appends a flight report to the 'Code Build: Flight Recorder' output channel after every turn. Use this to separate model latency from CB host/webview tax.

codeBuild.mcpServers.description: MCP servers passed to ACP backends (Grok, OpenCode, Cline) on session/new. Each item: { name, command, args? }. When this setting is left unset, Code Build injects the default personal-browser stack (chrome-devtools --autoConnect + playwright) so agents can use the live Chrome profile via CDP. Set explicitly to [] to pass no MCP servers (disables default injection). Prefer codeBuild.disableDefaultMcpServers for a discoverable off switch.

codeBuild.disableDefaultMcpServers.description: When true, do not inject the default chrome-devtools + playwright MCP servers on ACP session/new. Explicit codeBuild.mcpServers entries (if set) are still passed through; with no override, an empty list is sent (no default npx spawns).

codeBuild.kpMcp.enabled.description: Inject the knowledge-planning MCP server (kp mcp: kp_search/kp_create/kp_link_session/…) into ACP sessions (Grok, Codex) with provenance env (KP_AGENT/KP_MODEL/KP_SESSION). Requires codeBuild.kp.command and codeBuild.kp.root; if either is missing the session starts without kp tools. Independent of the default browser MCP servers — works even with codeBuild.disableDefaultMcpServers or codeBuild.mcpServers: []. A user-supplied 'kp' entry in codeBuild.mcpServers always wins (no duplicate injected).

codeBuild.kp.command.description: Absolute path to the knowledge-planning CLI entry script (e.g. /Users/me/projects/knowledge-planning/src/cli/index.ts). Spawned as `<node> <this path> mcp` — set the script path, not 'node'.

codeBuild.kp.root.description: Planning store root passed to the kp MCP server as KP_ROOT (e.g. /Users/me/docs/planning).

scripts.build: npm run build:webview && npm run build:host
